projects.title: PLANET OF THE APES: LAST FRONTIER
projects.summary: Enterprise-scale production with IP constraints most studios never face. Learned to solve problems inside systems you can't just rebuild — delivering across multiple titles with strict brand and quality bars.

expanded.problem: Disney IP titles operate under constraints most game studios never encounter — brand guidelines, legal review pipelines, cross-department approvals — all while maintaining shipping velocity.
expanded.solution: Developed tooling and workflow patterns that allowed rapid iteration within strict constraints. Built editor extensions that automated compliance checks, reducing review cycle time significantly.
expanded.outcome: Shipped multiple titles across platforms while maintaining Disney's quality and brand standards. Built internal reputation for finding creative solutions within seemingly rigid systems.

expanded.problem: Disney IP titles operate under constraints most game studios never encounter — brand guidelines, legal review pipelines, cross-department approvals — all while maintaining shipping velocity.
expanded.solution: Developed tooling and workflow patterns that allowed rapid iteration within strict constraints. Built editor extensions that automated compliance checks, reducing review cycle time significantly.

projects.title: RAW DATA
projects.summary: Room-scale VR combat had no established playbook. Helped build the systems framework that made physical VR combat feel credible — defining what action games in VR could be. One of the first successful room-scale multiplayer titles on the market.

expanded.problem: Room-scale VR combat had no established conventions. Everything from locomotion to weapon feel to spatial audio had to be invented from scratch in a medium that was weeks old.
expanded.solution: Built core gameplay systems including physics-driven melee, IK-resolved body presence, and a spatial audio combat framework. Iterated constantly with playtests to dial in the feel that made Raw Data feel real rather than gimmicky.
expanded.outcome: Raw Data became one of the first VR titles to hit $1M in revenue. It set a bar for room-scale action that studios are still referencing today.

projects.title: IRON PACT
projects.summary: Content updates took two weeks to balance and ship. Built a data-driven pipeline that cut it to three days without touching the build. 2.4M downloads, 4.7★ rating.

expanded.problem: The content team needed 2+ weeks per balance update, requiring a full engineer to touch the build each time. At live-service velocity, that was unsustainable.
expanded.solution: Architected a fully data-driven content pipeline — all game balance, economy values, and content gating driven from remote JSON configs. Built a custom editor dashboard for non-engineers to author, validate, and push updates without a code deploy.
expanded.outcome: Update cycle dropped from 14 days to 3. The content team became self-sufficient. 2.4M downloads and a 4.7★ App Store rating across iOS and Android.

projects.title: NEON DEPTHS
projects.summary: WebGL builds were bloated and slow. Engineered a custom streaming pipeline and WASM compression strategy to hit sub-8s load time. Game Jam 1st place.

expanded.problem: Unity WebGL builds were landing at 80MB+ with 30+ second load times on average connections — a death sentence for a game jam entry that needs to hook players in seconds.
expanded.solution: Audited the full asset pipeline, implemented addressable asset streaming, stripped unused engine modules, and applied Brotli compression to WASM. Rebuilt the loading screen as a playable micro-experience to mask remaining load time.
expanded.outcome: Final build: under 12MB. Load time: sub-8 seconds on average connections. Placed 1st overall in the jam across 340+ entries.
